payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields typically mean: token counts record prompt/input tokens, completion/output tokens, and total tokens used by the run; provider/model IDs identify the serving provider and exact model/version used so results are reproducible and costs can be mapped correctly; estimated price is the calculated cost for the run, usually derived from token counts, provider/model pricing, and sometimes cached-token or tool-use rates; latency p50, p90, and p95 are percentile latency metrics across requests, where p50 is the median response time, p90 is the time under which 90% of requests complete, and p95 is the time under which 95% of requests complete. These latency fields should specify units, usually milliseconds, and the measurement window or sample set when available.

entity_resolution.reason: The request asks for an explanation of evaluation accounting fields and does not provide a durable personal fact to store.

details: Explained latency percentile fields p50, p90, and p95.
